behaviors.description: Review analysis focuses only on changed lines within the diff, not the entire file content
behaviors.justification: The diff parsing and scoping logic must correctly filter to only changed lines. A unit test with a mocked diff could pass even if the real code requests full file content from GitHub or fails on realistic diff structures (merge commits, binary files, etc.). Testing requires exercising the actual filtering logic against realistic diff structures that match GitHub's format.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test PR that modifies 3 of 10 lines in a file. Run the review analysis with the actual diff structure. Verify the analysis processes only the 3 changed lines by checking logs, intermediate data structures, or coverage reports. Repeat with PRs containing different change patterns (insertions, deletions, multiple files) to ensure the filtering works across realistic diffs.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test requires realistic diff structures and verification of actual filtering behavior—a hand-crafted unit test could miss errors that surface only with real GitHub diff formats.

behaviors.description: Inline review comments are posted to the exact diff positions corresponding to identified issues
behaviors.justification: GitHub's API requires a 'position' parameter (1-indexed position in the diff, not line number in the file). Mocking the API call could always succeed even if position calculation is wrong—the real failure is comments appearing at wrong lines in the PR view. This requires realistic diff data to verify the position-to-line mapping works correctly for edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test PR with changes at multiple line numbers, including edge cases like insertions at the start, deletions, and context lines. For each identified issue, verify that the inline comment's position parameter correctly maps to the actual diff position. Capture the API calls (via mock or real) and verify position values match the expected diff positions. Validate by checking comment placement in a test PR interface.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires Integration—realistic diff structures to exercise position calculation, verification of GitHub API position parameters, and ideally validation that comments appear at correct locations.

behaviors.description: The code review agent identifies at least one real bug in a pull request that contains a known introduced defect
behaviors.justification: The ability to find bugs depends entirely on the real LLM's reasoning capability. A mocked LLM would trivially pass. Only a real LLM can demonstrate whether the system can identify defects. The outcome may vary slightly across runs due to non-determinism, making this inherently an Agentic test where the tool itself is the critical variable.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test PR with a clear, intentional bug (e.g., missing null-check, off-by-one error, unhandled exception). Run the code review agent with a real LLM against the modified code. Capture all findings. Verify at least one finding correctly identifies the bug using keyword matching or semantic similarity. Run the test multiple times (minimum 5 runs) to verify consistency of bug detection across LLM invocations.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test explicitly requires a real LLM, making it Agentic. Using mocked LLMs or snapshots would not test the actual capability.

behaviors.description: Every finding in the top-level summary comment has a corresponding inline comment posted at the same location
behaviors.justification: This tests data consistency and orchestration between two outputs (inline comments and summary comment). The failure mode is likely a logic error in deciding which findings to post where. Mocking GitHub API and using a fixed LLM response allows testing this coordination logic without real LLM non-determinism. A pure unit test would not catch errors that surface when running the full pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full agent pipeline with a test PR containing multiple identifiable issues. Mock the GitHub API to capture all posted comments (inline and summary). Extract the findings list from the summary comment. For each finding in the summary, verify an inline comment exists with matching content at a corresponding location. Repeat with 3+ test PRs with different issue patterns. Flag any summary findings without inline comments as failures.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires Integration—full local pipeline execution with realistic data flow between components. Could be Unit if testing only the consistency-check logic in isolation, but real failures emerge from full pipeline coordination.

behaviors.description: The complete workflow—reading the PR diff, analyzing with the LLM, posting inline comments, and creating a summary—executes successfully against a real pull request without manual intervention
behaviors.justification: The requirement explicitly states 'a real pull request', implying real GitHub API integration is non-negotiable. Real GitHub API calls expose failures that mocked infrastructure would hide: authentication issues, rate limits, API version mismatches, endpoint changes. The workflow also involves a real LLM (Agentic) orchestrated with multiple steps. System-level testing is the minimum to verify infrastructure reliability; Workflow level adds full pipeline coordination validation.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test GitHub repository and a PR with intentional code issues. Run the code review agent against the real PR on the test repository. Monitor execution for completion without errors. Verify inline comments appear at correct positions in the actual PR interface. Confirm a summary comment is posted. Validate that the entire sequence completes without manual fixes (no re-runs, no manual API corrections). Log all GitHub API calls made. Test with both small (1-file) and larger (5+ file) PRs.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires real GitHub API calls (System level), real LLM execution (Agentic level), and orchestration of multiple steps in sequence (Workflow potential). The explicit requirement for 'a real pull request' confirms System-level infrastructure is essential.
